response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong track record in web, mobile, and low-latency services, but his profile does not align with the Principal Performance Architect role. He has no demonstrated experience in SoC or IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or deep-learning workload optimization — which are the core technical pillars of this position. While his low-latency work at Sociomantic and some embedded/hardware-adjacent projects at Altom show depth in performance-conscious engineering, they fall far short of the hardware-software co-design and AI accelerator expertise required at the principal level.
strengths: 14+ years of software engineering experience with a consistent track record of performance-conscious, low-latency systems work (e.g., millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic using async I/O and DLang)
Broad polyglot and systems-level background including C/C++, DLang, and embedded work (NI LabWindows, robotic arms), showing capability beyond web-only engineering
Meets the educational/experience threshold: BS in Computer Science with well over 8 years of professional experience
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, which is a core required skill and the primary technical domain of this role
No background in AI/ML, deep neural network training or inference, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator workloads — the central focus of the team
No hardware-software co-design experience at the chip or accelerator level; hardware exposure is limited to peripheral tooling and robotics control software

key_factors.factor: No AI/deep-learning or GPU architecture background
Low-latency systems and performance engineering experience
